channel.title: Frontaliere Ticino — English
channel.description: News and guides for cross-border workers in Ticino

item.title: Varese - Soroptimist Varese funds a scholarship for a study on pulmonary fibrosis in memory of Maria Vittoria Tenconi
item.description: The biennial scholarship of 50,000 euros has been awarded to Caterina Barbieri for research on pulmonary fibrosis.

item.title: Smuggling Mozzarella in Ticino: How to Conduct the Right Research?
item.description: Discover how to conduct the right research to import mozzarella into Switzerland without any issues.

item.title: Security Agent Struck in Cadenazzo: Serious Injuries
item.description: A security agent was struck by a car in Cadenazzo, Ticino. His life is in danger.

item.title: Easter Tourism in Ticino Promises Pleasant Surprises
item.description: Bookings have exceeded 60% and hospitality operators are enthusiastic.

item.title: Federal Administrative Court Rejects Final Appeals Against Lugano Tram-Treno Project
item.description: The Federal Administrative Court has rejected the final appeals against the Lugano Tram-Treno project

item.title: Lake Como: Tourist Coaches and Line Buses Under Pressure - Diesel Costs +40 Million Euros/Month
item.description: The diesel crisis is hitting hard the public and tourist transportation around Lake Como, with additional costs of 40 million euros per month.

item.title: Complementary Examination on the Catwalk: Pre-Enrollments Now Open
item.description: The Department of Education, Culture and Sport informs that pre-enrollments are now open for the course of preparation for the complementary examination on the catwalk 2026/2027.

item.title: Transfer of Chiasso Post Office
item.description: The transfer of the Chiasso post office could be an opportunity for the territory

item.title: Chiasso Takes Action Against the Tiger Mosquito: Analysis of the 2026 Strategy
item.description: The municipality of Chiasso is tackling the threat of the tiger mosquito with a new strategy. Andrea Mini (PLR) questions the municipality for an effective response.

item.title: Permessi di dimora: diverse opinioni sulla consultazione del casellario giudiziale
item.description: The Canton Ticino would like to be able to verify criminal records at every request for a residence permit, but the Federal Council is holding back.

item.title: Doubtful Permits, Roveredo Unhappy and Worried About Chur's Response
item.description: The municipality of Roveredo criticizes the risk assessment for residence permits and requests a change in procedure.

item.title: The Customs Office of Chiasso Prepares to Become a Modern Technological Hub
item.description: The customs office of Chiasso, a meeting point between goods and people, is evolving with innovative projects and advanced technologies.

item.title: Lombardy Drives Italian Footwear Export Growth
item.description: In 2025, Lombardy records a turnover of €12.8 billion in the footwear sector, with a growing export of 7.9%.

item.title: Ticino and Lombardy: manufacturing system is the backbone of the economy
item.description: Regional councillor Licata supports the Lombardy and Ticino manufacturing systems

item.title: In Ticino, criminal investigations increase in 2025
item.description: In 2025, 363 new criminal investigations were launched in Switzerland, marking a +9.3% rise compared to 2024. Terrorism and economic crimes are among the main causes.

item.title: Truck Breakdown at San Gottardo: Traffic Halted in Ticino
item.description: A truck broke down at San Gottardo, causing a complete standstill of traffic in both directions. The situation has affected numerous cross-border workers and travelers passing through.

item.title: Como and Lake Como for Easter is a Boom: 'Bookings Over 90% in City'. But Be Wary: 'Summer Fears'
item.description: Easter in Como and Lake Como is a boom: bookings over 90%. However, there are fears for the summer.

item.title: San Gottardo, traffic jam since Holy Thursday: no surprise, plenty of patience
item.description: The San Gottardo highway in peak season: slowdowns and traffic jams in the direction of Ticino and Italy

item.title: International mobility of nursing staff on the rise
item.description: The Nursing Up union denounces the exodus of nurses from Italy to Switzerland

item.title: With almost 70 kilograms of mozzarella in Ponte Tresa: a new case of smuggling
item.description: An Italian driver was stopped in Ponte Tresa with over 67 kilograms of undeclared mozzarella. Another case of smuggling was recorded in Mendrisio

item.title: Seventy kilograms of mozzarella in the SUV's trunk. Double customs seizure in Ticino
item.description: Two distinct cases of food contraband were intercepted on the same day by UDSC collaborators between Sottoceneri and Sopraceneri.

item.title: Silver Smuggling: Seven Minted Silver Pieces Seized
item.description: The Swiss customs authority has seized seven minted silver pieces during a routine inspection.

item.title: Limiting the Population in Ticino to 10 Million: Challenges and Opportunities
item.description: The initiative by the Swiss People's Party (UDC) aims to set a cap of 10 million inhabitants in Switzerland by 2050, with direct implications for the Canton of Ticino and cross-border workers.

item.title: Nighttime Roadwork on Via Lavizzari in Chiasso
item.description: From April 7 to 18, 2026, Via Lavizzari in Chiasso will be affected by nighttime roadwork. The road will be closed to regular traffic from 20:00 to 05:00.

item.title: Doctor Prescribes Cannabis for Chronic Disorder, Health Insurance Won't Pay
item.description: The article reports on the case of a patient who received a prescription for cannabis from a doctor for a chronic disorder, but the health insurance did not cover the expense.

item.title: Ten years of discussion on taxation and cross-border workers
item.description: The Switzerland-Italy Congress returns to Varese to discuss new tax and cross-border worker developments

item.title: Switzerland short on eggs: customs quota increased
item.description: The Swiss Federal Council has decided to increase the customs quota for eggs by 71%, bringing it to 36,000 tonnes.

item.title: Ticino: 20,000 signatures against new fees for home care
item.description: The petition 'Non è colpa dei pazienti' has gathered over 20,000 signatures in just 5 days in opposition to the new charges for home care services

item.title: Return of the Italian ambassador to Bern after controversy over the Crans-Montana case
item.description: Gian Lorenzo Cornado is preparing to attend a commemoration in Crans-Montana, marking an important step in the thaw between Italy and Switzerland. Judicial cooperation is strengthening.

item.title: Precious Silver Coins Hidden in Car: Man Fined at Ponte Chiasso Border Crossing
item.description: A man was fined for hiding seven precious silver coins in his car at the Ponte Chiasso border crossing.

item.title: New alliance for the safety of Lake Maggiore
item.description: Italy and Switzerland join forces to protect the lake environment

item.title: Tries to pull a fast one at the border between Como and Switzerland: 7 kg of rare pure silver coins found in Mustang
item.description: Italian citizen stopped at the Ponte Chiasso crossing with 7 kg of rare silver coins hidden in the trunk of a Ford Mustang

item.title: ICT Workforce in Ticino: ATED Calls for Representation in the Tripartite Commission
item.description: The Ticino ICT sector, with over 14,000 professionals and an economic impact of approximately 1.4 billion Swiss francs, is demanding a direct role in the decision-making process of the cantonal labor market.

item.title: Chiasso Will Not Introduce Meal Tax to Preschool
item.description: The Chiasso Municipal Council has rejected the proposal to introduce a meal tax to preschool.

item.title: Trade Unions in Ticino: 'Article 42 Should Be Applied in the Leonardo Cascina Costa Contract Change'
item.description: Trade unions from Filt Cgil, Fit Cisl and Uiltrasporti are calling for a meeting at the Prefecture to discuss the dispute.

item.title: School Meal Tax Introduced in Chiasso Asylum
item.description: The municipality of Chiasso has introduced a tax on school meals for nursery school students.

item.title: Works on the Italian railway network: here's what changes for TILO
item.description: From April 3rd, TILO S30 connections will undergo significant changes in circulation due to infrastructure works on the railway line between Pino Tronzano and Luino.

item.title: Stopped with too much tripe
item.description: A 50-year-old man from the Mendrisiotto region was stopped and fined for exceeding the limit of transportable meat.

item.title: Tariff Negotiations: 'The March 31 Deadline Is No Longer Valid'
item.description: This was confirmed by the President of the Confederation, Guy Parmelin, who stated that it is worth continuing negotiations to reach an agreement between Switzerland and the United States on tariffs.

item.title: Ticino innovates with smart glasses for the visually impaired
item.description: The startup Lighthouse Tech presents TAMI, glasses with radar that vibrate to signal obstacles. Positive tests from people with low vision and winner of the Unitas Innovation Award.

item.title: Parmelin: negotiations with the US beyond March 31 for the commercial agreement
item.description: The deadline set for the end of the month is no longer valid due to the decision of the Supreme Court of the United States.

item.title: Code at the San Gottardo
item.description: Ten kilometres of queue at the north portal of the San Gottardo, waiting times up to one hour and thirty minutes

item.title: A9: closures between Chiasso and Como, impact on cross-border commuters
item.description: New closures on the A9 highway between Chiasso and Como from March 30 to April 2, 2026. Here are the alternatives for cross-border commuters.

item.title: Brawl in Ponte Tresa
item.description: Two people were hospitalized after a violent brawl in Ponte Tresa overnight.

item.title: Health Insurance Fund: Let's Apply the Lega dei Ticinesi Initiative Immediately
item.description: The Lega dei Ticinesi is calling for the full tax deductibility of health insurance premiums. The Federal Council has announced its plan: partial application of the initiatives and implementation conditional on financial coverage. ⚠️

item.title: Construction Work on Magliaso's New Primary School Has Begun
item.description: The new primary school in Magliaso will be ready for the 2028/2029 academic year

item.title: Brawl in the night in Lavena Ponte Tresa, two foreigners in hospital
item.description: A night-time brawl in Lavena Ponte Tresa has left two foreigners in hospital. The police are on the scene to investigate.

item.title: Underground Work in Varesotto, 46 Cases Discovered in a Few Months
item.description: 46 Cases of Underground Work Discovered in a Few Months in Varesotto

item.title: Francisca Trail, from Lavena Ponte Tresa
item.description: The walk for the 10th anniversary of the Francisca del Lucomagno trail has started from Lavena Ponte Tresa

item.title: Europe Takes Action: Spain Cuts Fuel Prices by 30 Cents per Litre
item.description: Spain Becomes First European Country to Cut Fuel Prices Amid Rising Energy Costs.
